segments.text: For the sons of Korah. A psalm.
LORD, you were favorable to your land; you restored the fortunes of Jacob.
segments.speaker: Psalmist

footnotes: for the sons: or, of the sons

segments.text: You forgave the iniquity of your people; you covered all their sin.
segments.speaker: Psalmist

segments.text: You withdrew all your wrath; you turned from your hot anger.
segments.speaker: Psalmist

footnotes: thyself…: or, thine anger from waxing hot

segments.text: Restore us again, O God of our salvation, and put away your indignation toward us.
segments.speaker: Psalmist

segments.text: Will you be angry with us forever? Will you prolong your anger to all generations?
segments.speaker: Psalmist

segments.text: Will you not revive us again, that your people may rejoice in you?
segments.speaker: Psalmist

segments.text: Show us your steadfast love, O LORD, and grant us your salvation.
segments.speaker: Psalmist

segments.text: Let me hear what God the LORD will speak, for he will speak peace to his people, to his saints; but let them not turn back to folly.
segments.speaker: Psalmist

segments.text: Surely his salvation is near to those who fear him,
that glory may dwell in our land.
righteousness and peace kiss each other.
Faithfulness springs up from the ground,
and righteousness looks down from the sky.
Yes, the LORD will give what is good,
and our land will yield its increase.
and make his footsteps a way.
